$schema: OIO Apex brand tokens — extracted from oio-apex-brand-guide.html and HANDOFF.md. Single source of truth for all video components; nothing downstream should re-derive a brand value.

rust.role: vintage/patina only — not a mood pick

flag.role: confirmation/pricing signal only — not a mood pick

condensedBlack.role: display/punch only, never below H2, no italic ever
condensedBlack.note: Possibly orphaned per HANDOFF.md — hero punch moved to wide Helvetica Bold/Black. Still used for corner-label numerals/leaderboard digits per this project's design direction.

signPainter.role: vintage script, hero sizes only (120-160px), flat color only — no gradient/outline/drop-shadow

cornerLabel.rule: left = fact (year/make/model or event category), right = name/sub-fact. Box always sits on the outer frame edge. Box always contrasts with its own background: white box on a dark shot, black box on a light shot. Unboxed side matches the frame color, not the box.

social.note: Sizes are % of export width (cqw) so badge/label stay the same real size across every export aspect — see brand guide section 06 and SocialFrame.tsx. Badge/label doubled from the original 96px/30px-at-1080 figures per Ian, 2026-07-18 — original numbers read too small against real feed posts. cornerLabelFontSize brought back down to 3.2cqw the same day: at 5.56cqw the doubled size clipped long real content ("1982 HONDA PRELUDE") into an ellipsis inside the 36cqw maxPartWidth box — 3.2cqw is the largest size that fits that real caption content with no ellipsis, measured via a Playwright binary search against the actual CornerLabel/SocialFrame render, not eyeballed. badgeDiameter halved again same day (17.8cqw -> 8.9cqw) per Ian, reviewing a real headless-rendered card — the doubled size read too big once seen at real export resolution instead of in Storybook's preview scale. This value is the single source of truth. A separate standalone Social Post Maker artifact (published at claude.ai, used for mobile posting — NOT retired, Ian actively uses it) has its own independently-hacked local copy of this badge math (13.35cqw as of 2026-07-18) that was never updated to match this file's later halving to 8.9cqw, and has no embedded-font fix or square/wide aspect support either — it is a known-drifted third copy of this brand math, not a mirror of it. Don't assume the artifact matches tokens.json without checking; resyncing it was out of scope for the 2026-07-18 session that wrote this note.
social.gridCropSafety: Tried raising badgeOffset/cornerLabelOffset to 6cqw/9.5cqw on 2026-07-18 to survive Instagram's profile-grid center-crop (see git history for the math). Ian reverted same day — read as too far inset from the corner at the original offset's intended scale. Back to the shared 2.22cqw both elements had before. Profile-grid crop vulnerability (portrait/wide lose the badge+label entirely when IG squares them for the grid view) is therefore a known, accepted tradeoff, not unresolved — don't re-attempt the same fix without asking first.
